title: Polypodium refulgens
taxonomy: Polypodium refulgens is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. This family is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that produce spores. Within the Polypodiaceae family, Polypodium refulgens is further classified as a species of the genus Polypodium. The genus Polypodium is a large and diverse group of ferns, with many species found throughout the world. Polypodium refulgens is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America.
uses: Polypodium refulgens is used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in Asia and Latin America. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various ailments such as arthritis and skin conditions. It is also used to reduce fever and relieve pain. Additionally, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and improve circulation. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential side effects of using Polypodium refulgens for medicinal purposes.
